query_normalized: predicting battery electrolyte decomposition products via dft and machine learning chemistry
rationale: The paper is a general survey on "learning curves" as a meta-learning concept for algorithm selection and resource budgeting, which is distinct from the user's specific domain of predicting chemical decomposition products using DFT and ML. It fails to address the specific mechanism (electrolyte decomposition), the domain (battery chemistry), or the variables (DFT-derived features and decomposition products), falling under the rejection rule for "Distinct construct sharing only homonym keywords"
